headline: The CPA spike is real, not an attribution artefact; frequency confirms the fatigue read.

findings.verdict: delivery is over-serving the same prospecting pool (freq 4.8 vs 3.1)
findings.why: Frequency +55% on prospecting is the delivery-side fingerprint of the same fatigue the creative lane sees. One aging asset is being shown too often to too few.

findings.verdict: CPA rise survives the blended check — not a windowing artefact
findings.why: Per analytics, platform CPA is inflated; but blended CPA itself moved +22% and ROAS -21%, and Google brand (cleanly attributed) held. A measurement glitch wouldn't spare brand and hit blended. The deterioration is genuine.

findings.verdict: respect Wed-9am-ET launch slot at go-live
findings.why: No new intraday anomaly this week; defer to the established best-slot learning when scheduling the test rather than launching cold on a weekend.

implication: Timing/attribution corroborate the creative diagnosis and rule out 'it's just measurement.' Frequency cap + fresh creative will relieve delivery pressure.
source: analytics / Attribution + UTM (blended-CAC check) + ads / frequency & fatigue thresholds
